title: More Than $116 Million Has Already Been Bet on the Oscars
text: Betting on the 2026 Oscars reached unprecedented levels, with over 116 million dollars wagered across prediction markets including Kalshi and Polymarket. This surpasses previous years' activity and reflects growing mainstream acceptance of prediction markets. The Best Picture race between One Battle After Another and Sinners drove much of the trading volume, with both markets showing consistent odds throughout the season. Prediction markets provide real-time odds that aggregate thousands of individual bettors' assessments of likely outcomes. For the 2026 Oscars, professional bettors, film industry insiders, and casual fans all contributed to the price discovery process. The heavy betting activity validated the traditional awards tracking methodology, with guild awards proving highly predictive of Oscar outcomes. One Battle After Another's domination of guild awards translated to approximately 75-77% odds on prediction markets throughout the final week of voting. Sinners' SAG Award victory briefly moved its odds upward, demonstrating how late developments impact market prices. This dynamic pricing mechanism creates opportunities for sophisticated bettors to identify arbitrage opportunities across different prediction platforms. The transparency and immediacy of prediction markets has made them an increasingly valuable forecasting tool for the entertainment industry.
